name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
يَـٰبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَتِىَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَنْعَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَأَوْفُوا۟ بِعَهْدِىٓ أُوفِ بِعَهْدِكُمْ وَإِيَّـٰىَ فَٱرْهَبُونِ (٤٠)
```

```english
O Children of Israel, remember My blessing which I bestowed upon you, and fulfill My covenant that I may fulfill your covenant, and be in awe of Me [alone]. (40)
```

```arabic
وَءَامِنُوا۟ بِمَآ أَنزَلْتُ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا مَعَكُمْ وَلَا تَكُونُوٓا۟ أَوَّلَ كَافِرٍۭ بِهِۦ ۖ وَلَا تَشْتَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِى ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا وَإِيَّـٰىَ فَٱتَّقُونِ (٤١)
```

```english
And believe in that which I have sent down confirming that which is with you, and do not be the first ones to deny it, and do not sell My signs for a paltry gain, and be wary of Me [alone]. (41)
```

```arabic
وَلَا تَلْبِسُوا۟ ٱلْحَقَّ بِٱلْبَـٰطِلِ وَتَكْتُمُوا۟ ٱلْحَقَّ وَأَنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (٤٢)
```

```english
Do not mix the truth with falsehood, nor conceal the truth while you know. (42)
```

```arabic
وَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتُوا۟ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَٱرْكَعُوا۟ مَعَ ٱلرَّٰكِعِينَ (٤٣)
```

```english
And maintain the prayer, and give the zakat, and bow along with those who bow [in prayer]. (43)
```

```arabic
۞ أَتَأْمُرُونَ ٱلنَّاسَ بِٱلْبِرِّ وَتَنسَوْنَ أَنفُسَكُمْ وَأَنتُمْ تَتْلُونَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ (٤٤)
```

```english
Will you bid others to piety and forget yourselves, while you recite the Book? Do you not exercise your reason? (44)
```

## (Commentary)

The Glorified began His rebuke of the Jews, spanning over a hundred verses, in which He mentions the blessings He bestowed upon them and the honors He granted them. He contrasts these with their response of disbelief (الكفر), disobedience (العصيان), breaking of the covenant (نقض الميثاق), rebellion (التمرد), and denial (الجحود). He reminds them by referencing twelve accounts from their history, such as their deliverance from the House of Pharaoh by the parting of the sea, the drowning of Pharaoh and his hosts, the appointment at Mount Tur, their taking of the calf as an object of worship after him, Moses’ command to them to slay themselves, and their demand that Moses show them Allah openly—whereupon the thunderbolt seized them before Allah the Exalted resurrected them—down to the last of the stories alluded to, all of which are replete with divine favors (ألطاف إلهيَّة) and Lordly cares (عنايات ربانية). He also reminds them of the covenants (المواثيق) He took from them, which they subsequently broke and cast behind their backs. Furthermore, He reminds them of the acts of disobedience they committed, the crimes they perpetrated, and the sins their hearts earned despite the prohibitions in their Book and the explicit deterrents of their intellects—all due to the hardness of their hearts, the wretchedness of their souls, and the straying of their endeavors.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَأَوْفُوا بِعَهْدِي** — **"and fulfill My covenant"** (2:40). The root of the word covenant (العهد) is "preservation" or "safekeeping" (الحفاظ). From this root, its various meanings are derived, such as covenant in the sense of a pact (الميثاق), an oath (اليمين), a testament (الوصية), a meeting (اللقاء), a dwelling (المنزل), and similar meanings.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَإِيَّايَ فَارْهَبُونِ** — **"and be in awe of Me [alone]"** (2:40). Awe (الرهبة) is fear (الخوف), and it is the opposite of desire (الرغبة).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَلَا تَكُونُوا أَوَّلَ كَافِرٍ بِهِ** — **"and do not be the first to defy it"** (2:41). That is, do not be the first among the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب), or among your own people from those who have passed and those to come; for the disbelievers of Mecca had already preceded them in disbelieving in it.
